claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. Her name is Alex, and she's 8 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

To start, do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Now, does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she does. Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! That's helpful information. 

Just to confirm - is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex? (Only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Just one more question to complete the assessment:

Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year? (This won't affect your eligibility to receive Child Benefit, but it may mean you need to pay a tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the requirements:

✅ Alex is under 16 years old (she's 8)  
✅ You live in the UK  
✅ Alex lives with you, so you're responsible for her  
✅ Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her  

**Additional information:**
- Since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you won't need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge
- You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the government's official channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant meets all the key eligibility criteria: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (she is 8 years old), (2) the claimant lives in the UK, (3) Alex lives with the claimant, making them responsible for her care, and (4) nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and all other requirements are met, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex. Additionally, as neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, they will not be subject to the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old daughter Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they live in the UK, Alex lives with them (making them responsible for her), Alex is under 16, and no one else is claiming for her. There are no complications such as the child being in care or hospital, and the High Income Child Benefit tax charge does not apply as neither parent earns over £60,000.

Since only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and all other requirements are met, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex. Additionally, as neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, they will not be subject to the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they live in the UK, Alex lives with them (making them responsible for her), Alex is under 16, and no one else is claiming for her. There are no complications such as the child being in care or hospital, and the High Income Child Benefit tax charge does not apply as neither parent earns over £60,000.
